title: To John, Bishop of Syracuse.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Our son the glorious exconsul Leontius has made a serious complaint to us of our brother and fellow-bishop Leo; and his complaint has altogether disturbed us, since a bishop ought not to have acted so precipitately and lightly.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: This case we have committed, to be thoroughly enquired into, to our Guardian (defensoris) Romanus when he comes to you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, the messenger who was sent by him (i.e. by Leontius) complains of your Fraternity, that in the defence of the illustrious physician Archelaus the interests of our brother and fellow-bishop, the Metropolitan Domitian, suffer damage.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed your Fraternity ought justly to protect your sons, or it may be in this case the interests of holy Church, and to give no occasion for evil-speaking to adversaries.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I doubt not, however, even while thus speaking, that you do take heed to this: yet we have enjoined on the same Romanus, when he comes to you, to arrange with you what is right with regard to this case also.
